title: Projection operator onto spin-S eigenspaces of total and orbital angular momenta
abstract: The Frobenius covariant is used to construct a projection operator onto the spin-S eigenspaces associated with the squares of the total and orbital angular momenta. The covariant admits two equivalent representations: as a polynomial in powers of the scalar product of the spin and orbital angular momentum operators, and as a finite expansion in terms of their respective polarization operators. A correspondence is established with Villars' angular momentum projection, used in nuclear structure studies.
summary: This paper develops an algebraic method to construct projection operators for total and orbital angular momentum. By providing an expansion in terms of polarization operators, it allows for the efficient separation of radial and angular components in calculations involving higher-spin particles in symmetric potentials.
main_problem: The construction of an efficient projection operator onto the simultaneous spin-S and orbital angular momentum eigenspaces to simplify matrix element calculations.
main_result: The derivation of a rotation-covariant projection operator expressed as both a polynomial in the scalar product of spin and orbital angular momentum and a finite expansion of polarization operators.
method: The use of the Frobenius covariant and algebraic expansion techniques involving irreducible tensor operators and polarization operators.
model_or_system: Particles with spin-S in spherically symmetric potentials, with applications ranging from nuclear shell models to relativistic particles after 3D reduction.
key_observables: Transition matrix elements, decay rates, magnetic dipole moments, and electric quadrupole moments.
important_parameters: Quantum numbers J, L, S, and M; particle energy E; and spatial coordinates r, r'.
main_assumptions: The derivation assumes spherical symmetry in the potential or reference frame.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of angular momentum projection, develops the Frobenius covariant approach, provides two equivalent algebraic representations, establishes a connection to Villars' projection, and demonstrates applicability to higher-spin particles and relativistic reductions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
